Used userId as PK (not generic), no access pattern table, SK has entity prefixes (USER#, ORDER#) but PK is specific. Line items denormalized. 1 GSI for status. No entity chart, no IaC, TTL/billing not explicit. No Scan (good). Violates should-NOT on userId as PK.

Uses tenant#{tenantId} PK for users/projects but tasks use project#{projectId} as PK breaking co-location. 4 GSIs (exceeds should-NOT of 3). No GSI overloading. GSI3 assignedUserId could leak across tenants. No access pattern table, no entity chart/IaC, no PAY_PER_REQUEST. No Scan.
Produced in-memory JavaScript data structure instead of DynamoDB schema. Asked clarifying questions about tech stack and persistence. No DynamoDB design attempted — total miss on the task.

Flat appointmentId PK with 4 separate GSIs instead of adjacency list pattern. No inverted GSI. Date queries handled via GSI3/GSI4. Single table (good). No Scan. No duplicate items. But 4 GSIs violates should-NOT of <=2.

Uses unbucketed SENSOR#id PK (no time-bucketing). ISO timestamp SK. TTL 90 days correctly set. Latest reading via ScanIndexForward=false Limit=1. Streams+Lambda for alerting. No access pattern table. No hot partition warning despite high-frequency writes. Violates should-NOT on unbucketed partition.

Created 3 separate tables (Organizations, Users, Invoices) mirroring SQL structure. Line items embedded in invoice (good denormalization). GSI for unpaid invoices. Denorm tradeoff warnings present. But multi-table design assumes JOINs. No access pattern table. No entity chart. No migration discussion.

Created 10 separate tables instead of single-table design. All 15 patterns addressed but not listed in access pattern table. Mentions ElastiCache for trending but no OpenSearch recommendation. Has tradeoff discussion. Violates should-NOT: 10 tables with multiple GSIs each (far exceeds 5+ GSIs). No access pattern table.
v2 improved from 5/8. Feed fanout section helped. Only missed: groups_patterns (partial).
v2 improved from 6.5/9. Migration section helped. Denorm tradeoffs now partial instead of fail.

Perfect score. Time-bucketed PK (SENSOR#id#HOUR#YYYY-MM-DDTHH), READING# timestamp SK, TTL 90 days (7776000s), latest reading via descending query with fallback, Streams+Lambda for alerting, explicit hot partition warning, no scan, entity chart. Major improvement over v2 (was 8/9).

Strong adjacency list design. Appointments under DOCTOR# PK, GSI1 for patient perspective, GSI2 for global date queries. 0.5 deduction on inverted_gsi: uses custom GSI1PK=PATIENT#<id> rather than canonical GSI1PK=SK,GSI1SK=PK inversion. No duplicate items, no scan, 2 GSIs.

Perfect score. Monthly bucketed PKs (SENSOR#id#YYYY-MM), TS# SK, 90-day TTL, separate #LATEST item, DynamoDB Streams with event filter for alerting, hot partition addressed via bucketing, 1 sparse GSI, full SAM template.

Perfect score. All 15 patterns listed and grouped. Trending explicitly offloaded to Redis/OpenSearch. Feed fan-out-on-write vs read vs hybrid discussed with celebrity threshold. Only 1 GSI (overloaded across 4 entity types). Explicit tradeoff tables for denormalization and unbounded collections.

Near-perfect. Collapsed 4 SQL tables into single DynamoDB table. Invoice+line items colocated under INV# PK. Sparse GSI2 for unpaid invoices with attribute removal on payment. Denorm tradeoff warnings (org name changes need Streams). 0.5 deduction: migration_discussion lacks explicit parallel-run or cutover validation strategy.
